{{wikidata link|Q11634949}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2014-10-27 (Mon) 11:43 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo Shrine1.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi|Q125546383|WD=Q125546383|1=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi}}Anabuto 4 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto |社格=former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=6th century |本殿= |例祭=October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kisogawa-chō (former {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Town|Q6416838|WD=Q6416838|1=Kisogawa, Aichi}} in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is Anabuto Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (another theory identifies Anabuto Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine (Yanagizu, Gifu)|Q11443286|WD=Q11443286|lt=Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} * Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}) === History === The founding period is uncertain, but tradition places it in the 6th-century reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} ({{ill|lt=539|Q32891|WD=Q32891|1=539}}-{{ill|lt=571|Q30475|WD=Q30475|1=571}}). In {{ill|lt=1090|Q19466|WD=Q19466|1=1090}} ({{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} 4), Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto was merged and the name changed to Kamo Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} ({{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|WD=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} issued prohibitions against misconduct by warriors and provided protection[https://www.aichitabi.com/itimiya/kamo.html Kamo Shrine]. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} donated shrine lands and built a protective cover over the shrine gate. A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. File:Kamo Shrine3.jpg|Haiden File:Kamo Shrine5.jpg|Plaque in the haiden written by {{ill|lt=Matsui Iwane|Q353901|WD=Q353901|1=Iwane Matsui}} File:Kamo Shrine2.jpg|Shrine gate said to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Kishin mask (craft) === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Old shrine gate (building) * Noh masks (one male and one female face) (sculpture) * Two swords (craft) * Ancient mirror (craft) * Ancient mirror (chrysanthemum-scatter twin-sparrow mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower butterfly twin-phoenix mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower twin-phoenix octagonal mirror) * Two kishin masks, Muromachi period * Female mask, Muromachi period * Sacred bell * Wooden seated monk statue, Muromachi period * Spring water of Tamanoi (historic site) === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: Kisogawa/Kitagata course, get off at "Kamo Shrine North" stop. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Tamanoi Station|Q7680699|WD=Q7680699|1=Tamanoi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === References === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Kishin mask (craft) === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Old shrine gate (building) * Noh masks (one male and one female face) mirror (auspicious-flower twin-phoenix octagonal mirror) * Two kishin masks, Muromachi period * Female mask, Muromachi period * Sacred bell * Wooden seated monk statue, Muromachi period * Spring water of Tamanoi (historic site) === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: Kisogawa/Kitagata course, get off at "Kamo Shrine North" stop. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Tamanoi Station|Q7680699|WD=Q7680699|1=Tamanoi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === References === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata It is Anabuto Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (another theory identifies Anabuto Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine (Yanagizu, Gifu)|Q11443286|WD=Q11443286|lt=Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} * Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}) === History === The founding period is uncertain, but tradition places it in the 6th-century reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} ({{ill|lt=539|Q32891|WD=Q32891|1=539}}-{{ill|lt=571|Q30475|WD=Q30475|1=571}}). In {{ill|lt=1090|Q19466|WD=Q19466|1=1090}} ({{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} 4), Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto was merged and the name changed to Kamo Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} ({{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|WD=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} issued prohibitions against misconduct by warriors and provided protection[https://www.aichitabi.com/itimiya/kamo.html Kamo Shrine]. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} donated shrine lands and built a protective cover over the shrine gate. A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. It is Anabuto Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (another theory identifies Anabuto Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine (Yanagizu, Gifu)|Q11443286|WD=Q11443286|lt=Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} * Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}) === History === The founding period is uncertain, but tradition places it in the 6th-century reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} ({{ill|lt=539|Q32891|WD=Q32891|1=539}}-{{ill|lt=571|Q30475|WD=Q30475|1=571}}). In {{ill|lt=1090|Q19466|WD=Q19466|1=1090}} ({{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} 4), Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto was merged and the name changed to Kamo Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} ({{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|WD=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} issued prohibitions against misconduct by warriors and provided protection[https://www.aichitabi.com/itimiya/kamo.html Kamo Shrine]. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} donated shrine lands and built a protective cover over the shrine gate. A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. File:Kamo Shrine3.jpg|Haiden File:Kamo Shrine5.jpg|Plaque in the haiden written by {{ill|lt=Matsui Iwane|Q353901|WD=Q353901|1=Iwane Matsui}} File:Kamo Shrine2.jpg|Shrine gate said to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Kishin mask (craft) === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Old shrine gate (building) * Noh masks (one male and one female face) (sculpture) * Two swords (craft) * Ancient mirror (craft) * Ancient mirror (chrysanthemum-scatter twin-sparrow mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower butterfly twin-phoenix mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower twin-phoenix octagonal mirror) * Two kishin masks, Muromachi period * Female mask, Muromachi period * Sacred bell * Wooden seated monk statue, Muromachi period * Spring water of Tamanoi (historic site) === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: Kisogawa/Kitagata course, get off at "Kamo Shrine North" stop. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Tamanoi Station|Q7680699|WD=Q7680699|1=Tamanoi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === References === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha {{wikidata link|Q11634949}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2014-10-27 (Mon) 11:43 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo Shrine1.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi|Q125546383|WD=Q125546383|1=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi}}Anabuto 4 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto |社格=former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=6th century |本殿= |例祭=October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kisogawa-chō (former {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Town|Q6416838|WD=Q6416838|1=Kisogawa, Aichi}} in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is Anabuto Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (another theory identifies Anabuto Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine (Yanagizu, Gifu)|Q11443286|WD=Q11443286|lt=Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} * Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}) === History === The founding period is uncertain, but tradition places it in the 6th-century reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} ({{ill|lt=539|Q32891|WD=Q32891|1=539}}-{{ill|lt=571|Q30475|WD=Q30475|1=571}}). In {{ill|lt=1090|Q19466|WD=Q19466|1=1090}} ({{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} 4), Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto was merged and the name changed to Kamo Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} ({{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|WD=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} issued prohibitions against misconduct by warriors and provided protection[https://www.aichitabi.com/itimiya/kamo.html Kamo Shrine]. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} donated shrine lands and built a protective cover over the shrine gate. A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. File:Kamo Shrine3.jpg|Haiden File:Kamo Shrine5.jpg|Plaque in the haiden written by {{ill|lt=Matsui Iwane|Q353901|WD=Q353901|1=Iwane Matsui}} File:Kamo Shrine2.jpg|Shrine gate said to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Kishin mask (craft) === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Old shrine gate (building) * Noh masks (one male and one female face) (sculpture) * Two swords (craft) * Ancient mirror (craft) * Ancient mirror (chrysanthemum-scatter twin-sparrow mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower butterfly twin-phoenix mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower twin-phoenix octagonal mirror) * Two kishin masks, Muromachi period * Female mask, Muromachi period * Sacred bell * Wooden seated monk statue, Muromachi period * Spring water of Tamanoi (historic site) === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: Kisogawa/Kitagata course, get off at "Kamo Shrine North" stop. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Tamanoi Station|Q7680699|WD=Q7680699|1=Tamanoi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === References === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha {{wikidata link|Q11634949}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2014-10-27 (Mon) 11:43 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo Shrine1.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi|Q125546383|WD=Q125546383|1=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi}}Anabuto 4 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto |社格=former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=6th century |本殿= |例祭=October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kisogawa-chō (former {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Town|Q6416838|WD=Q6416838|1=Kisogawa, Aichi}} in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is Anabuto Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (another theory identifies Anabuto Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine (Yanagizu, Gifu)|Q11443286|WD=Q11443286|lt=Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} * Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}) === History === The founding period is uncertain, but tradition places it in the 6th-century reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} ({{ill|lt=539|Q32891|WD=Q32891|1=539}}-{{ill|lt=571|Q30475|WD=Q30475|1=571}}). In {{ill|lt=1090|Q19466|WD=Q19466|1=1090}} ({{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} 4), Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto was merged and the name changed to Kamo Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} ({{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|WD=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} issued prohibitions against misconduct by warriors and provided protection[https://www.aichitabi.com/itimiya/kamo.html Kamo Shrine]. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} donated shrine lands and built a protective cover over the shrine gate. A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. File:Kamo Shrine3.jpg|Haiden File:Kamo Shrine5.jpg|Plaque in the haiden written by {{ill|lt=Matsui Iwane|Q353901|WD=Q353901|1=Iwane Matsui}} File:Kamo Shrine2.jpg|Shrine gate said to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Kishin mask (craft) === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Old shrine gate (building) * Noh masks (one male and one female face) (sculpture) * Two swords (craft) * Ancient mirror (craft) * Ancient mirror (chrysanthemum-scatter twin-sparrow mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower butterfly twin-phoenix mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower twin-phoenix octagonal mirror) * Two kishin masks, Muromachi period * Female mask, Muromachi period * Sacred bell * Wooden seated monk statue, Muromachi period * Spring water of Tamanoi (historic site) === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: Kisogawa/Kitagata course, get off at "Kamo Shrine North" stop. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Tamanoi Station|Q7680699|WD=Q7680699|1=Tamanoi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === References === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q11634949}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2014-10-27 (Mon) 11:43 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo Shrine1.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi|Q125546383|WD=Q125546383|1=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi}}Anabuto 4 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto |社格=former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=6th century |本殿= |例祭=October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kisogawa-chō (former {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Town|Q6416838|WD=Q6416838|1=Kisogawa, Aichi}} in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is Anabuto Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (another theory identifies Anabuto Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine (Yanagizu, Gifu)|Q11443286|WD=Q11443286|lt=Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} * Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}) === History === The founding period is uncertain, but tradition places it in the 6th-century reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} ({{ill|lt=539|Q32891|WD=Q32891|1=539}}-{{ill|lt=571|Q30475|WD=Q30475|1=571}}). In {{ill|lt=1090|Q19466|WD=Q19466|1=1090}} ({{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} 4), Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto was merged and the name changed to Kamo Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} ({{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|WD=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} issued prohibitions against misconduct by warriors and provided protection[https://www.aichitabi.com/itimiya/kamo.html Kamo Shrine]. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} donated shrine lands and built a protective cover over the shrine gate. A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. {{wikidata link|Q11634949}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2014-10-27 (Mon) 11:43 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo Shrine1.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi|Q125546383|WD=Q125546383|1=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi}}Anabuto 4 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto |社格=former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=6th century |本殿= |例祭=October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kisogawa-chō (former {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Town|Q6416838|WD=Q6416838|1=Kisogawa, Aichi}} in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is Anabuto Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (another theory identifies Anabuto Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine (Yanagizu, Gifu)|Q11443286|WD=Q11443286|lt=Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} * Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}) === History === The founding period is uncertain, but tradition places it in the 6th-century reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} ({{ill|lt=539|Q32891|WD=Q32891|1=539}}-{{ill|lt=571|Q30475|WD=Q30475|1=571}}). In {{ill|lt=1090|Q19466|WD=Q19466|1=1090}} ({{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} 4), Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto was merged and the name changed to Kamo Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} ({{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|WD=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} issued prohibitions against misconduct by warriors and provided protection[https://www.aichitabi.com/itimiya/kamo.html Kamo Shrine]. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} donated shrine lands and built a protective cover over the shrine gate. A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. It is Anabuto Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (another theory identifies Anabuto Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine (Yanagizu, Gifu)|Q11443286|WD=Q11443286|lt=Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} * Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}) === History === The founding period is uncertain, but tradition places it in the 6th-century reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} ({{ill|lt=539|Q32891|WD=Q32891|1=539}}-{{ill|lt=571|Q30475|WD=Q30475|1=571}}). In {{ill|lt=1090|Q19466|WD=Q19466|1=1090}} ({{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} 4), Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto was merged and the name changed to Kamo Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} ({{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|WD=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} issued prohibitions against misconduct by warriors and provided protection[https://www.aichitabi.com/itimiya/kamo.html Kamo Shrine]. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} donated shrine lands and built a protective cover over the shrine gate. A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. File:Kamo Shrine3.jpg|Haiden File:Kamo Shrine5.jpg|Plaque in the haiden written by {{ill|lt=Matsui Iwane|Q353901|WD=Q353901|1=Iwane Matsui}} File:Kamo Shrine2.jpg|Shrine gate said to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Kishin mask (craft) === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Old shrine gate (building) * Noh masks (one male and one female face) (sculpture) * Two swords (craft) * Ancient mirror (craft) * Ancient mirror (chrysanthemum-scatter twin-sparrow mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower butterfly twin-phoenix mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower twin-phoenix octagonal mirror) * Two kishin masks, Muromachi period * Female mask, Muromachi period * Sacred bell * Wooden seated monk statue, Muromachi period * Spring water of Tamanoi (historic site) === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: Kisogawa/Kitagata course, get off at "Kamo Shrine North" stop. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Tamanoi Station|Q7680699|WD=Q7680699|1=Tamanoi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === References === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha {{wikidata link|Q11634949}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2014-10-27 (Mon) 11:43 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo Shrine1.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi|Q125546383|WD=Q125546383|1=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi}}Anabuto 4 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto |社格=former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=6th century |本殿= |例祭=October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kisogawa-chō (former {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Town|Q6416838|WD=Q6416838|1=Kisogawa, Aichi}} in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is Anabuto Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (another theory identifies Anabuto Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine (Yanagizu, Gifu)|Q11443286|WD=Q11443286|lt=Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} * Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}) === History === The founding period is uncertain, but tradition places it in the 6th-century reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} ({{ill|lt=539|Q32891|WD=Q32891|1=539}}-{{ill|lt=571|Q30475|WD=Q30475|1=571}}). In {{ill|lt=1090|Q19466|WD=Q19466|1=1090}} ({{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} 4), Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto was merged and the name changed to Kamo Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} ({{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|WD=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} issued prohibitions against misconduct by warriors and provided protection[https://www.aichitabi.com/itimiya/kamo.html Kamo Shrine]. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} donated shrine lands and built a protective cover over the shrine gate. A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. File:Kamo Shrine3.jpg|Haiden File:Kamo Shrine5.jpg|Plaque in the haiden written by {{ill|lt=Matsui Iwane|Q353901|WD=Q353901|1=Iwane Matsui}} File:Kamo Shrine2.jpg|Shrine gate said to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Kishin mask (craft) === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Old shrine gate (building) * Noh masks (one male and one female face) (sculpture) * Two swords (craft) * Ancient mirror (craft) * Ancient mirror (chrysanthemum-scatter twin-sparrow mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower butterfly twin-phoenix mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower twin-phoenix octagonal mirror) * Two kishin masks, Muromachi period * Female mask, Muromachi period * Sacred bell * Wooden seated monk statue, Muromachi period * Spring water of Tamanoi (historic site) === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: Kisogawa/Kitagata course, get off at "Kamo Shrine North" stop. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Tamanoi Station|Q7680699|WD=Q7680699|1=Tamanoi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === References === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha {{wikidata link|Q11634949}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2014-10-27 (Mon) 11:43 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo Shrine1.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi|Q125546383|WD=Q125546383|1=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi}}Anabuto 4 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto |社格=former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=6th century |本殿= |例祭=October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kisogawa-chō (former {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Town|Q6416838|WD=Q6416838|1=Kisogawa, Aichi}} in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is Anabuto Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (another theory identifies Anabuto Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine (Yanagizu, Gifu)|Q11443286|WD=Q11443286|lt=Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} * Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}) === History === The founding period is uncertain, but tradition places it in the 6th-century reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} ({{ill|lt=539|Q32891|WD=Q32891|1=539}}-{{ill|lt=571|Q30475|WD=Q30475|1=571}}). In {{ill|lt=1090|Q19466|WD=Q19466|1=1090}} ({{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} 4), Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto was merged and the name changed to Kamo Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} ({{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|WD=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} issued prohibitions against misconduct by warriors and provided protection[https://www.aichitabi.com/itimiya/kamo.html Kamo Shrine]. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} donated shrine lands and built a protective cover over the shrine gate. A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. File:Kamo Shrine3.jpg|Haiden File:Kamo Shrine5.jpg|Plaque in the haiden written by {{ill|lt=Matsui Iwane|Q353901|WD=Q353901|1=Iwane Matsui}} File:Kamo Shrine2.jpg|Shrine gate said to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Kishin mask (craft) === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Old shrine gate (building) * Noh masks (one male and one female face) (sculpture) * Two swords (craft) * Ancient mirror (craft) * Ancient mirror (chrysanthemum-scatter twin-sparrow mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower butterfly twin-phoenix mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower twin-phoenix octagonal mirror) * Two kishin masks, Muromachi period * Female mask, Muromachi period * Sacred bell * Wooden seated monk statue, Muromachi period * Spring water of Tamanoi (historic site) === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: Kisogawa/Kitagata course, get off at "Kamo Shrine North" stop. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Tamanoi Station|Q7680699|WD=Q7680699|1=Tamanoi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === References === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qq old]]

In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. File:Kamo Shrine3.jpg|Haiden File:Kamo Shrine5.jpg|Plaque in the haiden written by {{ill|lt=Matsui Iwane|Q353901|WD=Q353901|1=Iwane Matsui}} File:Kamo Shrine2.jpg|Shrine gate said to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Kishin mask (craft) === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Old shrine gate (building) * Noh masks (one male and one female face) (sculpture) * Two swords (craft) * Ancient mirror (craft) * Ancient mirror (chrysanthemum-scatter twin-sparrow mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower butterfly twin-phoenix mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower twin-phoenix octagonal mirror) * Two kishin masks, Muromachi period * Female mask, Muromachi period * Sacred bell * Wooden seated monk statue, Muromachi period * Spring water of Tamanoi (historic site) === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: Kisogawa/Kitagata course, get off at "Kamo Shrine North" stop. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Tamanoi Station|Q7680699|WD=Q7680699|1=Tamanoi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === References === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Qq old]]

{{wikidata link|Q11634949}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2014-10-27 (Mon) 11:43 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo Shrine1.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi|Q125546383|WD=Q125546383|1=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi}}Anabuto 4 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto |社格=former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=6th century |本殿= |例祭=October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kisogawa-chō (former {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Town|Q6416838|WD=Q6416838|1=Kisogawa, Aichi}} in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is Anabuto Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (another theory identifies Anabuto Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine (Yanagizu, Gifu)|Q11443286|WD=Q11443286|lt=Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} * Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}) === History === The founding period is uncertain, but tradition places it in the 6th-century reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} ({{ill|lt=539|Q32891|WD=Q32891|1=539}}-{{ill|lt=571|Q30475|WD=Q30475|1=571}}). In {{ill|lt=1090|Q19466|WD=Q19466|1=1090}} ({{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} 4), Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto was merged and the name changed to Kamo Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} ({{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|WD=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} issued prohibitions against misconduct by warriors and provided protection[https://www.aichitabi.com/itimiya/kamo.html Kamo Shrine]. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} donated shrine lands and built a protective cover over the shrine gate. A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|WD=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|WD=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. File:Kamo Shrine3.jpg|Haiden File:Kamo Shrine5.jpg|Plaque in the haiden written by {{ill|lt=Matsui Iwane|Q353901|WD=Q353901|1=Iwane Matsui}} File:Kamo Shrine2.jpg|Shrine gate said to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Kishin mask (craft) === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Old shrine gate (building) * Noh masks (one male and one female face) (sculpture) * Two swords (craft) * Ancient mirror (craft) * Ancient mirror (chrysanthemum-scatter twin-sparrow mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower butterfly twin-phoenix mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower twin-phoenix octagonal mirror) * Two kishin masks, Muromachi period * Female mask, Muromachi period * Sacred bell * Wooden seated monk statue, Muromachi period * Spring water of Tamanoi (historic site) === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|WD=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: Kisogawa/Kitagata course, get off at "Kamo Shrine North" stop. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Tamanoi Station|Q7680699|WD=Q7680699|1=Tamanoi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === References === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]]

A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|qid=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|qid=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|qid=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|qid=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|qid=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|qid=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|qid=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. File:Kamo Shrine3.jpg|Haiden File:Kamo Shrine5.jpg|Plaque in the haiden written by {{ill|lt=Matsui Iwane|Q353901|qid=Q353901|1=Iwane Matsui}} File:Kamo Shrine2.jpg|Shrine gate said to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Kishin mask (craft) === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Old shrine gate (building) * Noh masks (one male and one female face) (sculpture) * Two swords (craft) * Ancient mirror (craft) * Ancient mirror (chrysanthemum-scatter twin-sparrow mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower butterfly twin-phoenix mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower twin-phoenix octagonal mirror) * Two kishin masks, Muromachi period * Female mask, Muromachi period * Sacred bell * Wooden seated monk statue, Muromachi period * Spring water of Tamanoi (historic site) === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|qid=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: Kisogawa/Kitagata course, get off at "Kamo Shrine North" stop. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Tamanoi Station|Q7680699|qid=Q7680699|1=Tamanoi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|qid=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|qid=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === References === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]]

{{wikidata link|Q11634949}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2014-10-27 (Mon) 11:43 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo Shrine1.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi|Q125546383|qid=Q125546383|1=Kisogawa-chō Tamanoi}}Anabuto 4 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|qid=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto |社格=former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=6th century |本殿= |例祭=October 22 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Kamo Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kisogawa-chō (former {{ill|lt=Kisogawa Town|Q6416838|qid=Q6416838|1=Kisogawa, Aichi}} in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|qid=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is Anabuto Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (another theory identifies Anabuto Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine (Yanagizu, Gifu)|Q11443286|qid=Q11443286|lt=Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Shrine}} in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|qid=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}} * Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto (deity of {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|qid=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}) === History === The founding period is uncertain, but tradition places it in the 6th-century reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|qid=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} ({{ill|lt=539|Q32891|qid=Q32891|1=539}}-{{ill|lt=571|Q30475|qid=Q30475|1=571}}). In {{ill|lt=1090|Q19466|qid=Q19466|1=1090}} ({{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|qid=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} 4), Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto was merged and the name changed to Kamo Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|qid=Q19728|1=1184}} ({{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|qid=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|qid=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} issued prohibitions against misconduct by warriors and provided protection[https://www.aichitabi.com/itimiya/kamo.html Kamo Shrine]. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|qid=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} donated shrine lands and built a protective cover over the shrine gate. A great flood of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|qid=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} in June {{ill|lt=1586|Q6739|qid=Q6739|1=1586}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14) significantly changed the river's main channel and split {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|qid=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} in two. The region on the right bank became {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q11610214|qid=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}. Therefore, for parishioners in Hashima District, Mino Province, a branch shrine was established in 1599 ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4) at Minami-oyobi Village, Hashima District (present Masaki-chō Minami-oyobi, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|qid=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}). In January {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|qid=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), it was scheduled for promotion to prefectural shrine, but due to postwar {{ill|lt=Shinto Directive|Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}}, it was never ranked as kensha. === Precinct === The spring water that wells up in the precinct is called "Tamanoi Reisen" (Tamanoi spring / Tamai spring), and is the origin of the local place name "Tamanoi." This water is said to have healed the eye disease of {{ill|lt=Empress Kōmyō|Q837932|qid=Q837932|1=Empress Kōmyō}}. Faith in healing eye disease continues today. File:Kamo Shrine3.jpg|Haiden File:Kamo Shrine5.jpg|Plaque in the haiden written by {{ill|lt=Matsui Iwane|Q353901|qid=Q353901|1=Iwane Matsui}} File:Kamo Shrine2.jpg|Shrine gate said to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} File:Kamo Shrine6.jpg|Tamanoi sacred spring File:Kamo Shrine4.jpg|Ritual scene === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Kishin mask (craft) === Ichinomiya City designated tangible cultural properties === * Old shrine gate (building) * Noh masks (one male and one female face) (sculpture) * Two swords (craft) * Ancient mirror (craft) * Ancient mirror (chrysanthemum-scatter twin-sparrow mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower butterfly twin-phoenix mirror) * Ancient mirror (auspicious-flower twin-phoenix octagonal mirror) * Two kishin masks, Muromachi period * Female mask, Muromachi period * Sacred bell * Wooden seated monk statue, Muromachi period * Spring water of Tamanoi (historic site) === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|qid=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: Kisogawa/Kitagata course, get off at "Kamo Shrine North" stop. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Tamanoi Station|Q7680699|qid=Q7680699|1=Tamanoi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|qid=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|qid=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === References === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]]
